seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Testing Strategies problems in Instructor manifest as unreliable structured outputs from Pydantic-validated LLM responses, where agents fail to consistently extract complete data schemas despite retries. Common issues include exhausted validation retries, missing required fields in partial extractions, hallucinated enum values, and inconsistent nested object depths. These failures break agent workflows that depend on predictable structured data for decision-making and tool calling.

howItManifests.symptom: Instructor's `max_retries` exhausted after multiple validation failures
howItManifests.impact: Agent returns None or raw string instead of Pydantic model, breaking downstream logic

howItManifests.symptom: Pydantic model created but required fields remain None or missing
howItManifests.impact: Agent processes incomplete data, leading to incorrect actions or tool calls

howItManifests.scenario: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
howItManifests.symptom: Instructor validation passes invalid enum values not in Pydantic schema
howItManifests.impact: Agent uses hallucinated categories, corrupting business logic and data pipelines

howItManifests.scenario: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
howItManifests.symptom: Nested Pydantic models have varying depths across calls (sometimes missing sub-objects)
howItManifests.impact: Agent fails on type errors when accessing inconsistent nested structures

detection.name: Retry Exhaustion Monitor
detection.description: Detects when Instructor's validation retries are exhausted by catching None returns and checking retry metadata
detection.code:
from instructor import from_openai

from pydantic import BaseModel

class UserProfile(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

try:
    profile = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=UserProfile,
        max_retries=3,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract user profile"}]
    )
    print(f"✅ Valid profile: {profile}")
except Exception:
    print("❌ Validation failed - check retry exhaustion")

# Detection: Check if result is None after retries
if profile is None:
    print("🚨 DETECTED: Structured output validation retries exhausted")

detection.name: Missing Fields Validator
detection.description: Detects partial Pydantic models missing required fields using model validation state
detection.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError
from instructor import from_openai

class Order(BaseModel):
    id: str
    items: list[str]
    total: float

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

profile = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Order,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract order details"}]
)

# Detection: Check required fields explicitly
missing_fields = []
for field_name, field_info in Order.model_fields.items():
    if getattr(profile, field_name, None) is None and not field_info.is_optional():
        missing_fields.append(field_name)

if missing_fields:
    print(f"🚨 DETECTED: Partial extraction missing fields: {missing_fields}")

detection.name: Enum Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Validates enum fields against schema to catch hallucinated values slipping through
detection.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum
from instructor import from_openai

class StatusEnum(str, Enum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    SHIPPED = "shipped"
    DELIVERED = "delivered"

class Order(BaseModel):
    status: StatusEnum

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
order = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Order,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Order status: CANCELLED"}]
)

# Detection: Validate enum membership
if hasattr(order.status, '_value_'):
    valid_values = [e.value for e in StatusEnum]
    if order.status not in valid_values:
        print(f"🚨 DETECTED: Enum hallucination: '{order.status}' not in {valid_values}")

fixes.name: Strict Mode + Fallback Prompts
fixes.description: Enable strict mode and provide fallback system prompts for better extraction reliability
fixes.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel

class UserProfile(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int | None = None

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

# Fix: Strict mode + detailed system prompt
profile = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=UserProfile,
    strict=True,
    max_retries=5,
    mode='json',
    system_prompt="""You are a precise data extraction agent. 
    ONLY return valid JSON matching the schema exactly. 
    Never omit required fields. Use null for missing data.""",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract: John is 35 years old"}]
)

fixes.name: Nested Validation Cascade
fixes.description: Validate nested objects incrementally with partial=True and field-level retries
fixes.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class Address(BaseModel):
    street: str
    city: str | None = None

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    address: Address | None = Field(default=None, validate_default=True)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

# Fix: Partial validation for nested objects
user = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=User,
    partial=True,
    max_retries=3,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "John lives at 123 Main St, NYC"}]
)

# Validate nested structure
if user.address and not all([user.address.street, user.address.city]):
    print("Nested validation failed, retrying address only")

fixes.name: Enum Constraint Enforcement
fixes.description: Use custom validators and constrained enums to block hallucinated values
fixes.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, field_validator
from enum import Enum
from instructor import from_openai
from typing import Literal

ValidStatus = Literal["pending", "shipped", "delivered"]

class Order(BaseModel):
    status: ValidStatus

    @field_validator('status')
    @classmethod
    def validate_status(cls, v):
        valid = ["pending", "shipped", "delivered"]
        if v not in valid:
            raise ValueError(f"Status must be one of {valid}, got {v}")
        return v

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

# Fix: Literal types + custom validators
order = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Order,
    strict=True,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Order status update"}]
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically detects all four Instructor failure modes by wrapping `client.chat.completions.create()` calls with `vex.verify()`. It captures retry exhaustion via response metadata, validates Pydantic field completeness, scans enum values against schemas, and checks nested object consistency across agent runs. Vex provides rich observability dashboards showing failure rates by model, prompt, and schema complexity.

Remediation is automatic: Vex applies optimal `strict=True`, `max_retries`, and system prompts based on failure patterns observed. For hallucinated enums, Vex injects constraint-aware prompts. Nested structure issues trigger partial validation cascades. Production agents achieve 99.9% structured output reliability with zero manual prompt engineering.

Integration takes 2 lines of code and works with existing Instructor Pydantic models without modification.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Vex Integration - 2 lines
client = from_openai(OpenAI())
verified_client = verify(client)

class AgentAction(BaseModel):
    tool: str
    parameters: dict
    confidence: float

# All calls automatically monitored
response = verified_client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=AgentAction,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Book flight to NYC"}],
    max_retries=3
)

# Vex auto-detects: retry exhaustion, missing fields, enum hallucinations, nested inconsistencies
# Dashboards show: 98% success rate, 2% missing confidence field, remediation applied
print(f"Verified Agent Action: {response}")
